2. Use the BuildId to locate the correct symbols

I can try to download the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId, or look for them in the NuGet runtime packs that ship with .NET Android. arguments.command:
# Create a minimal ELF with the right build ID to feed to dotnet-symbol
# Using python to craft a minimal ELF arm64 binary with a specific build ID
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import struct

build_id_hex = "4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
build_id = bytes.fromhex(build_id_hex)

# Minimal ELF64 for aarch64
EI_MAG = b'\x7fELF'
EI_CLASS = 2  # 64-bit
EI_DATA = 1   # Little endian
EI_VERSION = 1
EI_OSABI = 0
EI_ABIVERSION = 0

# ELF header (64 bytes)
e_type = 3     # ET_DYN (shared object)
e_machine = 183  # EM_AARCH64
e_version = 1
e_entry = 0
e_phoff = 64   # Program header right after ELF header
e_shoff = 0
e_flags = 0
e_ehsize = 64
e_phentsize = 56
e_phnum = 1    # One PT_NOTE for build ID
e_shentsize = 64
e_shnum = 0
e_shstrndx = 0

elf_header = struct.pack('<4sBBBBB7xHHIQQQIHHHHHH',
    EI_MAG, EI_CLASS, EI_DATA, EI_VERSION, EI_OSABI, EI_ABIVERSION,
    e_type, e_machine, e_version, e_entry, e_phoff, e_shoff, e_flags,
    e_ehsize, e_phentsize, e_phnum, e_shentsize, e_shnum, e_shstrndx)

# Build the NT_GNU_BUILD_ID note
# Note: namesz=4 ("GNU\0"), descsz=len(build_id), type=3 (NT_GNU_BUILD_ID)
note_name = b'GNU\x00'
note_header = struct.pack('<III', len(note_name), len(build_id), 3)
note_data = note_header + note_name + build_id
# Pad to 8-byte alignment
while len(note_data) % 8 != 0:
    note_data += b'\x00'

# Program header for PT_NOTE
note_offset = 64 + 56  # After ELF header + 1 program header
PT_NOTE = 4
p_type = PT_NOTE
p_flags = 4  # PF_R
p_offset = note_offset
p_vaddr = 0
p_paddr = 0
p_filesz = len(note_data)
p_memsz = len(note_data)
p_align = 4

phdr = struct.pack('<IIQQQQQQ',
    p_type, p_flags, p_offset, p_vaddr, p_paddr, p_filesz, p_memsz, p_align)

with open('/tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(elf_header + phdr + note_data)

print(f"Created minimal ELF with build ID: {build_id_hex}")
print(f"File size: {64 + 56 + len(note_data)} bytes")
PYEOF

# Verify the build ID
file /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so
readelf -n /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so 2>/dev/null || echo "readelf not available"

EI_DATA = 1   # Little endian
EI_VERSION = 1
EI_OSABI = 0
EI_ABIVERSION = 0

# ELF header (64 bytes)
e_type = 3     # ET_DYN (shared object)
e_machine = 183  # EM_AARCH64
e_version = 1
e_entry = 0
e_phoff = 64   # Program header right after ELF header
e_shoff = 0
e_flags = 0
e_ehsize = 64
e_phentsize = 56
e_phnum = 1    # One PT_NOTE for build ID
e_shentsize = 64
e_shnum = 0
e_shstrndx = 0

elf_header = struct.pack('<4sBBBBB7xHHIQQQIHHHHHH',
    EI_MAG, EI_CLASS, EI_DATA, EI_VERSION, EI_OSABI, EI_ABIVERSION,
    e_type, e_machine, e_version, e_entry, e_phoff, e_shoff, e_flags,
    e_ehsize, e_phentsize, e_phnum, e_shentsize, e_shnum, e_shstrndx)

# Build the NT_GNU_BUILD_ID note
# Note: namesz=4 ("GNU\0"), descsz=len(build_id), type=3 (NT_GNU_BUILD_ID)
note_name = b'GNU\x00'
note_header = struct.pack('<III', len(note_name), len(build_id), 3)
note_data = note_header + note_name + build_id
# Pad to 8-byte alignment
while len(note_data) % 8 != 0:
    note_data += b'\x00'

# Program header for PT_NOTE
note_offset = 64 + 56  # After ELF header + 1 program header
PT_NOTE = 4
p_type = PT_NOTE
p_flags = 4  # PF_R
p_offset = note_offset
p_vaddr = 0
p_paddr = 0
p_filesz = len(note_data)
p_memsz = len(note_data)
p_align = 4

phdr = struct.pack('<IIQQQQQQ',
    p_type, p_flags, p_offset, p_vaddr, p_paddr, p_filesz, p_memsz, p_align)

with open('/tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(elf_header + phdr + note_data)

print(f"Created minimal ELF with build ID: {build_id_hex}")
print(f"File size: {64 + 56 + len(note_data)} bytes")
PYEOF

# Verify the build ID
file /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so
readelf -n /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so 2>/dev/null || echo "readelf not available"
